reviews.review_msg: Professor Flagel is not a bad prof. but, the course material can be quite boring. If you are not interested in 20th century British plays I recommend not taking Engl 113W and taking another 1st year English class.

Nadine is a great person, however, after about week 5 she stopped making slides which would make her lectures hard to pay attention to. On a couple occasions she would at last minute cancel lecture without warning. Do-able but I won't take a course with her again.

Nadine's lectures can be boring, but I liked the readings she chose and she provided good resources to help during the course which made it not too hard. She was very helpful during an office hour when I visited, and she genuinely cares about her students. Though I wish there weren't so many handouts, a lot of paper could have been saved!

I honestly dont get all the mean comments on here. Dr. Flagel is an excellent prof: she is professional, gives breaks, adds a personal touch, her lectures are fun, her tutorials are awesome/fun, and her one-on-one office hours are invaluable! If you are willing to put in the work you will do really well and learn important skills for the future.

Nadine assigns two different papers with vague instructions for you to interpret on your own but has her own expectations that are difficult to meet. Lectures are not important as she talks a lot about the author and not really anything about the text and how to interpret it.
She's not an approachable person and she isn't professional at work. She discusses her personal problems in class. She's disorganized and all over the place with her lectures.
Don't take Nadine Flagel, worst English Prof. Not at all helpful.
Do not recommend this class if you can't stand boring lectures.

Lectures are extremely boring and not useful at all. She cracks jokes that only she laughs at. Assignments are unclear. Not too many readings as we read 5 plays throughout the course which take about 2 hours each. All things considered, I would not recommend taking this class unless you are very interested in drama and are a good writer.

Nadine is a tad boring when giving lectures - she's the only one who laughs at her jokes. But you'll learn a lot from her class (eg fancy punctuation)! She's very considerate with deadlines and she gives detailed and helpful feedbacks. She also picks current issues to write about (ie sexual harassment on campus) which is pretty awesome.
tough marker but knowledgeable. i wouldn't take this course with her or any other.

I enjoyed the course a lot. The required readings were long but interesting, but avoid this course if you hate reading. Nadine is passionate about the course material and very caring to her students. Her grading is a bit tough but if you participate a lot and go to her office hours you'll be OK. Sit in front- her voice is rather quiet.

She assigns unrealistic readings, sometimes over 200 pages a week. Her lectures are really long and overly specific so it is hard to apply what she tells you to your assignments. She goes on many tangents during lecture as well. She is tough but willing to help.

Honestly, don't take this class!! By the end you will be crying... her final exam may actually make kids cry. Her teaching is nothing special and she basically wants you to memorize the text to answer any of her final exam questions. Not a fair representation of how much you know.

Be ready to read throughout the entire semester. Her lectures are dry and feel like you will die of old age in there. She is nice but can get hard to listen to after a long time. It is easy to get confused in her class; she tends to go off track multiple times. Overall, I would not take this class again.
Final exam is not clear, do not take this class. TAs are awesome though.

She is definitely a great person, very understanding and kind. She is a little but "different" but hey, thats always a little bit fun. Despite being a good person her lectures are SO LONG & boring, many absolutely dread them

Hard Marker (like hard for first year)  Boring Lectures (don't learn anything in it) Don't recommend Missed a lecture  Had to read a long book (David Copperfield) Please save yourself and don't take this class with her

Her lectures are boring and not useful at all. If you decide to skip lectures,just remember to pick someone else's tutorial, not her own. Your marker is your tutor, and all tutor are actually having highly independent standards and tastes. This makes lectures rather unimportant, unless you picked the one held by herself.
Very high expectations, and very opinionated. Too much material for a full-time student, prof missed lectures.

She's a great prof! She has taught me a lot literature wise. I do have to say that for a 100 level class, it is really heavy. She chose 4 great books for the semester and I found it hard to keep up with the readings (also had short stories) since some of them are long-800 pages. She is a strict marker but a kind, engaging, and understanding prof.
Simply put, she is the worst prof ever. Never take a class with her! She marks excruciatingly hard and is so boring
Pretty chill, reasonable prof, I'd take another course with her. Fair marking, not an easy A though.

WAY TOO hard of a marker for English 101, she expects your writing to be perfect otherwise you get a C. And she'll put lots of checkmarks on your papers so you think you are doing well then she smacks a big 64% on the paper and you are left more confused than ever.
She is pretty nice but quite strict and thorough on marking in-class writings.!

I took this class a few semesters back. She is an awesome professor, but the TA's for her class were terrible.  She was super nice and always available. She needed better TA's, as they didn't do very much - as most of them don't.
she's funny and sweet. marks fairly.

I tried so hard to be nice and form a rapport, and she was nothing but unpleasant. Her own opinions are the only right ones and she made it clear that if we disagreed we were wrong. She needs to realise that just because she dislikes something doesn't make us wrong for disagreeing. HORRIBLE prof. Avoid at all costs.

She definitely engages the students into the course by sharing her enthusiasm. Very helpful and open-minded. She has given me a much better experience in English. I know that some students have commented on how she seems to stick to her own answers but in English, as long as an answer is well supported, the prof usually accepts it (as with my case)

It is interesting how there is a series of comments with the same chatty, conversational style of writing that offer praise to Nadine while emphasizing what she expects from her students. Could it be Nadine pretending to be students? I am convinced it is, but you be the judge.

Classes were dry and it felt like I was in a bad re-run since classes were so repetitive. I'm an English Major and expect a hefty reading list,  but her expectations were far beyond manageable, even for a 300 level class. To add to this, the readings were BORING. For a class called 'Pop Lit', I expected something more exciting than what she offered
Relating everything in literature to your experience raising a child is not teaching.

Alright so basically  she is clear in articulating her argument and adheres to some of the student demands if something is difficult or troublesome.  Fair marker and fair tests, the only problem I found was that she talked a lot about her child and how motherly experience can be hard work but worth it. So, a bit sidetracked but a good prof overall.
Found this prof. very engaging.

Nadine disguises herself as a kindergarden teacher. Everything is cutesy and candy coated. Then she marks your essays and if you say anything that strays from her ideas about the novel you fail. This in a prof is deplorable.
Nadine is definately an intelligent person; however, I found her lectures to be quite repetitive and dry.

An unpleasant experience. Gives useful handouts and makes some insightful comments. However, she calls herself a "fair marker" but is very, very critical. Average for in-class essay was a C. Showed class a student's paper as a bad example. Time management could use some work-shows up late or gets sidetracked. I'm avoiding her for future classes.

Nadine is very knowledgeable on certain topics - but ONLY those topics. Your papers should echo her discussions in class or else it's hard to get a good grade. She comes off condescending but I think she's just socially awkward (but still condescending and pretentious). Compared to profs like Torsten or Ramsey, she has a long way to go.

Her lectures are very well thought through.  She is also a great speaker.  However, your assignments/essays have to mirror her own though, otherwise you are in trouble (mark wise, of course).  If you feel lucky, take the course - you might be her sister/borther in-thought. And indeed, you will gain a lot from her lecures (no sarcasm at all).

Nadine is wonderful... a little off-beat but endearingly so. She is very intelligent and well-spoken. Her lectures are nothing special but she seems to appreciate input from students and attempts to learn names of all the students.  She is down-to-earth and funny.

She's nice , but has yet to tell the class anything about literature a pompous fifteen year old couldn't have come up with. Also, says everything like it it the...most...important...thing... you've...ever... heard.
She looks like she's 15!Classes were painful to sit through. Who really wants to sit through scores of student presentations and class discussions? Four hours...painful!
Responsible.  Helpful.  Friendly.  Knowledgeable.  Finicky with grammar (Hey, it's an English class), gives interesting ideas, and shows relations of texts to history, politics, cultures, and arts.
I didn't like her style at all. She has really offbeat ideas about how to teach. She picks weird literature and makes her students to ridiculous activities and assignments

She confirmed for me that not all English professors are stuck up and above communicating with students.
 Her lectures were relevant, and she obviously cared about the learning process. Doesn't hesitate to tell students if they are on the wrong track and applauds you for having good input. Best prof I have had, wish she wasn't on mat. leave!!!

Nadine thinks she's great because she has her doctorate. seriously. I was so stoked for this class and she killed it. She handed our assignments back weeks late. She pretty much thought everything you had to say was stupid. She majorly had favorites. One of the worst teachers I've had for sure and I'm in my last semester.

Nadine only seems to like her own ideas and usually thinks your idea is, to put it simply, stupid, or not as good as hers. Agreeing with the comment below, she is hard to approach and does seem to think she is superior than the rest, especially in tutorial discussions. I also had a difficult time understanding what she was trying to communicate.

Although Nadine is intelligent and knows the material, she has difficulty explaining herself clearly, and often comes across in a condescending way when you ask for clarification. Even though I did well in this course, I found her lack of approachability disappointing.

Nadine obviously knows her literature. The amount of time she spends on preparing lecture material AND providing useful comments on assignments must be applauded. Do your readings, answer questions insightfully, mind your grammar, and you will be well on your way. For everyone who laments the reading list: Learn to read a course syllabus!

Why can't she run a course like someone normal? Why does she have such wierd taste in literature? Why is the reading list so random? Why didn't I drop this course when I thought about it after the first class?
Nadine was my prof for the first class I took at SFU.  I was really nervous about taking classes at a university level, but her class was interesting and fun.  I think she is a great prof.

Nadine is the most informative and interesting prof I've ever encountered.  Obviously engaged in the material, doesn't allow for much b.s. which is nice.  Anyone who really wants to learn about literature/history/culture should take one of her classes
mediocre teacher.  not enough clarity on assignments. very little class discussion. approachable and flexible. too inexperienced. learning contract a waste of a class.
Nadine is super pretty and a wonderful and dedicated teacher. Nice dresser as well!

Nadine is definitely not "hot", but interesting in a country-bumpkin sort of way.  Her lectures depend on class input, and she is fair with assignments and grading.  Even tho I received a good grade, she does play favorites and is very jealous of pretty girls in the class!Wo! She is passionate about her topics and tries to make a democratic class.
No Comments
Stay away from this new age instructor
Nice, but she's inexperienced. She relies way too much on student participation. If you want to be taught something, look elsewhere.

Nadine is awsome--her lectures and activities are relevant and thoughtful and she is a really kind and attractive person. It is too bad that the same person keeps giving her bad ratings. Seriously, get a life you loser. Don't you have anything better to do?

Nadine is an excellent prof. She's very helpful and encouraging, and her reading lists are wonderful. She sets a very comfortable, casual class environment and when you speak to her, its like talking to a peer instead of a prof.

Nadine is by far one of the best instructors I've encountered in my university experience.  She was not only informative and engaging, but also fun and easy to get along with.  Anyone interested in an English class that will goes beyond typical literary theories will love her teaching style.
Nadine is a great lecturer and is very passionate about the course material. She allows for creativity in her assignment and celebrates innovative ideas. I thoroughly enjoyed her class. A great prof.
The worst professor that I've had so far.

Nadine is fantastic. Her interest in course content is very high, as is her knowledge of course material. It's too bad some people had such strong objections too the assignments.  It sounds like someone didn't get a good grade. Boo Hoo.

Not the greatest lecture skills but the books and the course content are worthwhile. As mentioned the assignments can be very time consuming so be aware if taking a heavy course load or if you don't like creative projects.

Nadine is different as far as profs go, but she is extremely thoughtful and puts a lot of prep into her lectures.  Demands a lot of ingenuity  from students: a good experience if you have the time to put in.
No Comments

Nadine is an awsome prof.  Anyone who would criticize her experimental pedagogy, must simply not have understood it (although,I would have thought that grade-school crafts would have appealed to someone of such limited understanding)
Nadine is a great prof.

How she ever got hired in the first place is beyond me.  Anytime a requisite assignment for a 3rd-year English course involves doing a collage, the red flags have to be waving immediately.  Easily the worst "professor" I've ever had.
